2604.06799.title: Beyond Accuracy: Diagnosing Algebraic Reasoning Failures in LLMs Across Nine Complexity Dimensions
2604.06799.paper_summary: Algebraic reasoning remains one of the most informative stress tests for large language models, yet current benchmarks provide no mechanism for attributing failure to a specific cause. When a model fails an algebraic problem, a single accuracy score cannot reveal whether the expression was too deeply nested, the operator too uncommon, the intermediate state count too high, or the dependency chain too long. Prior work has studied individual failure modes in isolation, but no framework has varied each complexity factor independently under strict experimental control. No prior system has offered automatic generation and verification of problems of increasing complexity to track model progress over time. We introduce a nine-dimension algebraic complexity framework in which each factor is varied independently while all others are held fixed, with problem generation and verification handled by a parametric pipeline requiring no human annotation. Each dimension is grounded in a documented LLM failure mode and captures a structurally distinct aspect of algebraic difficulty, including expression nesting depth, simultaneous intermediate result count, sub-expression complexity, operator hardness, and dependent reasoning chain length. We evaluated seven instruction-tuned models spanning 8B to 235B parameters across all nine dimensions and find that working memory is the dominant scale-invariant bottleneck. Every model collapses between 20 and 30 parallel branches regardless of parameter count, pointing to a hard architectural constraint rather than a solvable capacity limitation. Our analysis further identifies a minimal yet diagnostically sufficient subset of five dimensions that together span the full space of documented algebraic failure modes, providing a complete complexity profile of a model's algebraic reasoning capacity.
2604.06799.paper_summary_zh: 代數推理仍然是對大型語言模型進行最具信息量的壓力測試之一，然而當前的基準測試並未提供將失敗歸因於特定原因的機制。當模型在代數問題上失敗時，單一的準確率分數無法揭示表達式是否過於深層嵌套、運算符是否過於不常見、中間狀態計數是否過高，或依賴鏈是否過長。先前的研究已孤立地研究了個別失敗模式，但沒有任何框架在嚴格的實驗控制下獨立變化每一個複雜性因素。沒有任何先前的系統提供自動生成和驗證逐漸增加複雜性的問題，以跟踪模型隨時間的進展。我們引入了一個九維代數複雜性框架，在該框架中，每個因素獨立變化，而其他所有因素保持固定，問題的生成和驗證由一個不需要人類標註的參數化管道處理。每個維度都基於已記錄的LLM失敗模式，並捕捉代數難度的結構上不同的方面，包括表達式嵌套深度、同時中間結果計數、子表達式複雜性、運算符難度和依賴推理鏈長度。我們評估了七個指令調整模型，涵蓋從8B到235B參數的所有九個維度，發現工作記憶是主導的尺度不變瓶頸。無論參數數量如何，每個模型在20到30個平行分支之間崩潰，這指向一個硬體架構限制，而非可解決的容量限制。我們的分析進一步確定了一個最小但診斷上足夠的五維子集，這五維子集共同涵蓋了已記錄的代數失敗模式的完整空間，提供了模型代數推理能力的完整複雜性概況。
2604.06799.author: Parth Patil et.al.
2604.06799.authors: Parth Patil, Dhruv Kumar, Yash Sinha, Murari Mandal
